docs.title: gqlforge
docs.text:
GraphQL client SDK generator for Go.

gqlforge orchestrates genqlient to generate type-safe Go client code from GraphQL schemas and operations.

docs.title: Key Concepts
docs.text:
Unlike OpenAPI (where the spec defines all operations), GraphQL requires two artifacts:

  1. Schema - The GraphQL type system (fetched via introspection)
  2. Operations - The specific queries/mutations your SDK exposes (user-defined)
GraphQL Endpoint
      ↓
gqlforge introspect
      ↓
schema.graphql
      +
operations/           ← You define these
  users.graphql
  projects.graphql
      ↓
gqlforge generate (wraps genqlient)
      ↓
generated/client.go

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/gqlforge/cmd/gqlforge@latest

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# 1. Initialize project structure
gqlforge init myapi

# 2. Introspect the GraphQL API
cd myapi
gqlforge introspect --token YOUR_TOKEN https://api.example.com/graphql

# 3. Define your operations (queries/mutations)
cat > operations/users.graphql << 'EOF'
query GetUser($id: ID!) {
  user(id: $id) {
    id
    name
    email
  }
}
EOF

# 4. Generate Go client
gqlforge generate

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Introspection - Fetch GraphQL schemas from any endpoint
  • SDL Export - Convert introspection results to Schema Definition Language
  • Authentication - Support for bearer tokens and goauth credentials
  • Code Generation - Generate type-safe Go clients via genqlient

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --creds <file> Path to goauth credentials file --account <key> Account key in credentials file -o, --output <dir> Output directory (default: .) -v, --verbose Enable verbose output

docs.title: introspect
docs.text:
Fetch a GraphQL schema via introspection.

gqlforge introspect [endpoint] [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --endpoint <url> GraphQL endpoint (alternative to positional arg) --token <token> Bearer token for authentication --sdl Output schema as SDL (.graphql) (default: true) --json Output raw introspection result (.json) --name <name> Base name for output files (default: schema)

Examples:

# Basic introspection
gqlforge introspect https://api.github.com/graphql --token ghp_xxx

# Using goauth credentials
gqlforge introspect --creds ~/.config/goauth/creds.json --account github \
    https://api.github.com/graphql

# Output both SDL and JSON
gqlforge introspect --sdl --json https://api.example.com/graphql

docs.title: init
docs.text:
Initialize a new gqlforge project.

gqlforge init <name> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --endpoint <url> GraphQL endpoint URL (saved to config) --package <name> Go package name for generated code (default: graphql)

Creates:

<name>/
├── genqlient.yaml        # genqlient configuration
├── schema.graphql        # GraphQL schema (run introspect to populate)
├── operations/           # Your GraphQL operations
│   └── example.graphql   # Example operation file
├── generated/            # Generated Go code (after running generate)
├── gqlforge.yaml         # Project configuration
└── .gitignore

Example:

gqlforge init myapi --package myapi --endpoint https://api.example.com/graphql

docs.title: scaffold
docs.text:
Generate stub GraphQL operations from schema types.

gqlforge scaffold <schema.graphql> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --type <type> Type to scaffold: Query, Mutation, or both (default: both) --depth <n> Max field depth for selection sets (default: 2) --include <pattern> Glob pattern for fields to include (comma-separated) --exclude <pattern> Glob pattern for fields to exclude (comma-separated) -o, --output <dir> Output directory (default: operations)

Examples:

# Generate all operations
gqlforge scaffold schema.graphql -o operations/

# Only queries with depth 3
gqlforge scaffold schema.graphql --type Query --depth 3

# Filter by field name
gqlforge scaffold schema.graphql --include "user*,feature*"
gqlforge scaffold schema.graphql --exclude "internal*"

docs.title: validate
docs.text:
Validate GraphQL operations against a schema.

gqlforge validate [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --schema <file> Path to schema file (default: schema.graphql) --operations <pattern> Glob pattern for operations (default: operations/*.graphql) --strict Treat warnings as errors --json Output validation results as JSON

Examples:

# Validate with defaults
gqlforge validate

# Custom paths
gqlforge validate --schema schema.graphql --operations "queries/*.graphql"

# Strict mode for CI/CD
gqlforge validate --strict

# JSON output
gqlforge validate --json

docs.title: diff
docs.text:
Compare local schema to remote endpoint (detect drift).

gqlforge diff <local-schema> <remote-endpoint> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --token <token> Bearer token for remote introspection --breaking-only Only show breaking changes --json Output diff as JSON

Examples:

# Basic diff
gqlforge diff schema.graphql https://api.example.com/graphql --token xxx

# Using goauth credentials
gqlforge diff schema.graphql --creds creds.json --account myapi \
    https://api.example.com/graphql

# Only breaking changes
gqlforge diff schema.graphql https://api.example.com/graphql --breaking-only

# JSON output for CI/CD
gqlforge diff schema.graphql https://api.example.com/graphql --json

Output:

Schema Diff: schema.graphql vs https://api.example.com/graphql

+ Added Types:
  + NewType

- Removed Types (BREAKING):
  - OldType

~ Changed Types:
  ~ User:
    + newField
    - removedField (BREAKING)

Summary: 1 added, 1 removed, 2 breaking changes

docs.title: watch
docs.text:
Watch files and auto-regenerate on changes.

gqlforge watch [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --config <file> Path to genqlient.yaml (default: genqlient.yaml) --debounce <duration> Debounce duration for rapid changes (default: 300ms)

Examples:

# Watch with defaults
gqlforge watch

# Custom config
gqlforge watch --config custom-genqlient.yaml

# Faster response
gqlforge watch --debounce 100ms

docs.title: generate
docs.text:
Generate Go client code using genqlient.

gqlforge generate [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --config <file> Path to genqlient.yaml (default: genqlient.yaml)

Prerequisites:

  1. Have a valid schema.graphql
  2. Define operations in operations/*.graphql
  3. Configure genqlient.yaml

Note: As of v0.2.0, genqlient is embedded and does not need to be installed separately.

docs.title: version
docs.text:
Print version information.

gqlforge version

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description GQLFORGE_TOKEN Bearer token for authentication GQLFORGE_ENDPOINT Default GraphQL endpoint URL

docs.title: Release Notes: v0.1.0
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-06-07

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: gqlforge is a GraphQL client SDK generator for Go that orchestrates genqlient to generate type-safe client code from GraphQL schemas and operations.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• GraphQL Introspection - Fetch schemas from any GraphQL endpoint
  • SDL Export - Convert introspection results to Schema Definition Language
  • goauth Integration - Flexible authentication via credentials file or bearer token
  • genqlient Workflow - Scaffold and generate type-safe Go clients

docs.title: Introspection Package
docs.text:
• GraphQL introspection client with full schema query
  • ToSDL() method for human-readable schema output
  • ToJSON() method for raw introspection export
  • Support for all GraphQL types: Object, Interface, Union, Enum, Input, Scalar

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text: Command Description introspect Fetch GraphQL schema via introspection query init Initialize genqlient project structure generate Generate Go client code using genqlient version Display version information

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Multiple authentication methods supported:

# goauth credentials file
gqlforge introspect --creds credentials.json --account myapi https://api.example.com/graphql

# Bearer token flag
gqlforge introspect --token YOUR_TOKEN https://api.example.com/graphql

# Environment variable
GQLFORGE_TOKEN=token gqlforge introspect https://api.example.com/graphql

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
GraphQL Endpoint
      ↓
gqlforge introspect
      ↓
schema.graphql
      +
operations/           ← You define these
  queries.graphql
      ↓
gqlforge generate (wraps genqlient)
      ↓
generated/client.go

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/gqlforge/cmd/gqlforge@latest

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Initialize project
gqlforge init myapi

# Introspect schema
cd myapi
gqlforge introspect --token YOUR_TOKEN https://api.example.com/graphql

# Define operations in operations/*.graphql
# Then generate client
gqlforge generate

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• github.com/spf13/cobra - CLI framework
  • github.com/grokify/goauth - Authentication

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Repository
  • genqlient - Upstream code generator

docs.title: Release Notes: v0.2.0
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-06-07

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This release adds powerful developer experience features: operation scaffolding, schema validation, drift detection, and file watching for automatic regeneration. The generate command now uses genqlient as an embedded library for better performance and error handling.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Scaffold Operations - Generate stub queries and mutations from schema types
  • Validate Operations - Check operations against schema before generation
  • Schema Drift Detection - Compare local schema to remote endpoint
  • Watch Mode - Auto-regenerate on file changes
  • Embedded genqlient - No longer requires genqlient CLI installation

docs.title: scaffold - Generate Stub Operations
docs.text:
Generate template operations from Query and Mutation types:

# Generate all operations
gqlforge scaffold schema.graphql -o operations/

# Only queries with depth 3
gqlforge scaffold schema.graphql --type Query --depth 3

# Filter by field name patterns
gqlforge scaffold schema.graphql --include "user*,feature*"
gqlforge scaffold schema.graphql --exclude "internal*,deprecated*"

Flags:

Flag Description Default --type Type to scaffold: Query, Mutation, or both both --depth Max field depth for selection sets 2 --include Glob patterns for fields to include --exclude Glob patterns for fields to exclude -o, --output Output directory operations

docs.title: validate - Validate Operations
docs.text:
Check operations against schema before generation:

# Validate with defaults
gqlforge validate

# Custom paths
gqlforge validate --schema schema.graphql --operations "queries/*.graphql"

# Strict mode (warnings become errors)
gqlforge validate --strict

# JSON output for CI/CD
gqlforge validate --json

Flags:

Flag Description Default --schema Path to schema file schema.graphql --operations Glob pattern for operations operations/*.graphql --strict Treat warnings as errors false --json Output as JSON false

docs.title: diff - Schema Drift Detection
docs.text:
Compare local schema to remote endpoint:

# Basic diff
gqlforge diff schema.graphql https://api.example.com/graphql --token xxx

# Only show breaking changes
gqlforge diff schema.graphql https://api.example.com/graphql --breaking-only

# JSON output for CI/CD
gqlforge diff schema.graphql https://api.example.com/graphql --json

Output:

Schema Diff: schema.graphql vs https://api.example.com/graphql

+ Added Types:
  + AiAgent
  + AiAgentPage

- Removed Types (BREAKING):
  - LegacyReport

~ Changed Types:
  ~ Feature:
    + newField
    - deprecatedField (BREAKING)
    ~ status: Status → StatusEnum (BREAKING)

Summary: 2 added, 1 removed, 3 breaking changes

docs.title: watch - Auto-Regeneration
docs.text:
Watch files and regenerate on changes:

# Watch with defaults
gqlforge watch

# Custom config
gqlforge watch --config genqlient.yaml

# Custom debounce
gqlforge watch --debounce 500ms

Flags:

Flag Description Default --config Path to genqlient.yaml genqlient.yaml --debounce Debounce duration 300ms

docs.title: Embedded genqlient
docs.text:
The generate command now uses genqlient as a library instead of calling it via exec.Command. This means:

  • No longer requires genqlient CLI to be installed
  • Better error messages with full context
  • Faster generation (no process spawning)
  • Version locked to go.mod

If you relied on a specific genqlient CLI version, note that gqlforge now uses genqlient v0.8.1.

docs.title: Updated Workflow
docs.text:
GraphQL Endpoint
      ↓
gqlforge introspect      ← Fetch schema
      ↓
schema.graphql
      ↓
gqlforge scaffold        ← NEW: Generate stub operations
      ↓
operations/*.graphql     ← Edit generated stubs
      ↓
gqlforge validate        ← NEW: Check before generation
      ↓
gqlforge generate        ← Now uses embedded genqlient
      ↓
generated/client.go

# For ongoing development:
gqlforge watch           ← NEW: Auto-regenerate on changes
gqlforge diff            ← NEW: Detect schema drift

docs.title: New Dependencies
docs.text:
• github.com/Khan/genqlient v0.8.1 - Embedded code generation
  • github.com/vektah/gqlparser/v2 v2.5.33 - Schema parsing and validation
  • github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify v1.10.1 - File system watching
  • github.com/bmatcuk/doublestar/v4 v4.10.0 - Glob pattern matching
